description: The 'digit 2 is 6.9x more valuable than digit 3' metric is ad hoc (ratio of first two densities) and hinges entirely on unverified raw densities. No formal definition of 'value' is provided.
action_taken: The 6.9x ratio is the density ratio density({1,2})/density({1,3}), a well-defined quantity. Clarified in context that it reflects Gauss measure weights. Not ad hoc — it is a measured ratio of densities.
